bio.education: Keyes to Learning Charter (Keyes, Calif.) ‘12 University of Alaska Fairbanks '18, Natural Resource Management
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Randy and Susan Maddalena
  • One sibling, Marty
  • Natural Resource Management major with a concentration in Forestry
  • Got involved with shooting through 4-H, which taught her organization and her grandpa, who taught her patience
  • Walked-on to one of the best rifle programs in the country at Alaska Fairbanks, and red-shirted her first season in 2013
  • To gain more match experience her coach, Dan Jordan, suggested she compete at the World Cup tryouts in May 2014, which ultimately proved successful as she earned the privilege to compete internationally
  • Enlisted in the U.S. Army in March 2019 and was assigned to the USAMU International Team as a shooter/instructor
Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, silver (50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women), 4th (10m Air Rifle - Women), 18th (10m Air Rifle - Team - Mixed)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 5th (50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – bronze (50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women), 8th (50m Rifle Prone - Women), 12th (50m Rifle Prone - Team (Mixed) - Mixed), 20th (10m Air Rifle - Team - Mixed), 26th (10m Air Rifle - Women)
  • Years of participation: 10m Air Rifle - Team - Mixed 2022, 2023; 10m Air Rifle - Women 2014, 2022, 2023; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women 2018, 2022, 2023; 50m Rifle Prone - Team (Mixed) - Mixed 2022, 2023; 50m Rifle Prone - Women 2022, 2023; 10m Air Rifle - Team - Women 2022; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Team - Mixed 2022; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Team - Women 2022
  • Medals: 3 (1 gold, 1 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 2022 (50m Rifle Prone - Team (Mixed) - Mixed)
    • Silver – 2022 (10m Air Rifle - Team - Women)
    • Bronze – 2023 (50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women)

Personal Website

Quick Facts

  • Graduated from Princeton University with a degree in English Literature
  • Worked in podcast production prior to pursuing a spot on the U.S. Olympic 3x3 Basketball Team

  • 3x3 World Cup, Vienna, 2023 (Team - Men)
  • Pan American Games, Santiago, 2023 (Team - Men)

    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 7th (Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Katlyn was born with Fibular Hemimelia which resulted in scoliosis and her right leg was amputated at 8-years-old
  • In 5th Grade, Katlyn was invited by her Special Education program to go to Alyeska Ski Resort
  • It was there that she joined an adaptive race program called Challenge Alaska, which she trained with throughout high school
  • Daughter of Jim Maddry and LeAnn Maddry
  • Katlyn has four siblings: Annaka, Elena, Hannah, and Joseph Maddry
  • Before she began snowboarding Katlyn participated in State chess competitions
  • Her favorite sporting moment was competing in the Dual Bank Slalom in 2021, "My riding was on fire, and I was just so happy to be there."
  • The first adaptive athlete she met was Keith Gabel in 2014 at the Hartford Ski Spectacular
  • The two have since become friends
  • Katlyn loves his positive attitude and his willingness to support her and other athletes on the slopes
  • Her favorite thing about snowboarding is that there's always room for improvement and new challenges to overcome
  • In three words, Katlyn would describe herself as: fun, loving, and a go-getter
  • She is known to her friends as Katy
  • Katy is a full-time student (undergoing an Architecture Degree), athlete and server at Buffalo Wild Wings.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Beijing 2022, 12th (Snowboard Cross - Lower Limb 2 Impaired - Women), 14th (Banked Slalom - Lower Limb 2 Impaired - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 4th (Banked Slalom - Lower Limb 2 Impaired - Women)
  • Years of participation: Banked Slalom - Lower Limb 2 Impaired - Women 2022
  • Top finish: 4th – 2022 (Banked Slalom - Lower Limb 2 Impaired - Women)

  • 2019 IBU Youth World Championships (Osrblie, Slovakia) - 83rd in individual / 73rd in sprint
  • 2018 Youth World Championships (Otepaa, EST) - 83rd in individual / 18th in relay / 70th in sprint
More
MORE ON LEXIE: Daughter of PJ Madigan and Melissa Madigan...Has one sister, Riley...Hobbies include swimming, running and playing soccer...Started competitive cross country skiing and biathlon in 7th grade and qualified for Junior Nationals in 8th grade as a second-year U14...One year later she qualified for Youth/Junior World Championships but didn't meet minimum age requirement to attend...Favorite athlete is Katie Ledecky because of her driven and hard-working personality both at school and at the pool...Is a member of the local swim team and competes in the spring and summer...she will never turn down the chance to hop in the pool for a workout.

bio.education: Nebraska City Senior High School (Nebraska City, Neb.)
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Mario and Mary Becerra
  • Mother to Reese and Malayna Madsen
  • Married Eric Madsen on June 2, 2001
  • Was left paralyzed by an unknown virus at age three
  • Began wheelchair racing in 1994 and qualified for her first Paralympic Games just two years later in Atlanta
  • First Native American female to win an Olympic exhibition bronze medal
  • Her 1996 Olympic and Paralympic pursuits were chronicled in a Nebraska Educational Television documentary entitled “The Cheri Becerra Story: God Made Her for This Sport”
  • Left racing for 13 years, returning to competition for the 2013 world championships in memory of her younger brother, Mario III, and their father, Mario Jr., who were killed in a car-train crash in 2007
  • Hobbies include scrapbooking, reading and watching her daughters compete in sports.
Paralympic Experience
  • 4-time Paralympian; 10-time Paralympic medalist (2 gold, 5 silver, 3 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (400m T54 - Women), bronze (100m T54 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, (4 x 400m Relay T53-54 - Women), silver (400m T54 - Women), 5th (100m T54 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Sydney 2000, gold (100m T54 - Women, 400m T54 - Women), silver (200m T54 - Women), 4th (800m T54 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Atlanta 1996, silver (100m T53 - Women, 200m T53 - Women), bronze (400m T53 - Women, 800m T53 - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – bronze (100m T54 - Women), 5th (400m T54 - Women)
  • Years of participation: 100m T54 - Women 2015, 2017, 2019; 400m T54 - Women 2013, 2015, 2017, 2019; 200m T54 - Women 2013, 2015, 2017; 800m T54 - Women 2013, 2015
  • Medals: 7 (3 silver, 4 bronze)
    • Silver – 2017 (100m T54 - Women, 200m T54 - Women); 2015 (200m T54 - Women)
    • Bronze – 2019 (100m T54 - Women); 2017 (400m T54 - Women); 2013 (200m T54 - Women, 400m T54 - Women)

bio.education: Fairborn Baker (Fairborn, Ohio) '78 United States Marine Corps
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Madsen has been to two different Paralympic Games in two sports--rowing and track and field
  • She won her first Paralympic medal in 2012 with bronze in the shot put
  • Madsen loves being around the water and was the only person in the world, male or female, to complete four ocean rowing crossings of three oceans and to have rowed a circumnavigation of Great Britain
  • She is also the only paraplegic to surf in the women’s world championships of longboard surfing in Biarritz, France, making the 2015 U.S. ISA adaptive surf team
  • Madsen runs a non-profit adaptive rowing program in Long Beach, Calif. and founded the California Adaptive Rowing Program in 1999
  • She wrote a book released in 2013 called "Rowing Against the Wind" and is a 20 year board member of the California Paralyzed Veterans
  • Madsen has five grandchildren.
Paralympic Experience
  • 3-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 7th (Javelin Throw F56 - Women), 8th (Shot Put F57 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, bronze (Shot Put F54-56 - Women), 5th (Javelin Throw F54/55/56 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Beijing 2008, 7th (Trunk & Arms Double Sculls - Mixed)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2015 – 6th (Shot Put F57 - Women), 8th (Javelin Throw F56 - Women)
  • Years of participation: Javelin Throw F56 - Women 2015; Shot Put F57 - Women 2015; Trunk & Arms Double Sculls - Mixed 2003, 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007; Trunk & Arms Single Sculls - Open 2002
  • Medals: 5 (4 gold, 1 silver)
    • Gold – 2006 (Trunk & Arms Double Sculls - Mixed); 2005 (Trunk & Arms Double Sculls - Mixed); 2004 (Trunk & Arms Double Sculls - Mixed); 2003 (Trunk & Arms Double Sculls - Mixed)
    • Silver – 2002 (Trunk & Arms Single Sculls - Open)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Freddy and Sheila Maguire
  • Has one sibling, Liam
  • Speaks Spanish
  • Hobbies include playing lacrosse, running and hiking
  • The 2016 Winter Youth Olympic Games will mark her first appearance on the international stage.

Career Highlights
  • World Junior Championships, Klínovec, 2017 (Snowboard Cross Team - Women, Snowboardcross - Women)
  • World Junior Championships, Rogla, 2016 (Snowboard Cross Team - Women, Snowboardcross - Women)

bio.fun_fact: Before each competition, he eats a chocolate bar
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Specialty: Men's Singles
  • Son of Tatyana Malinina and Roman Skornyakov
  • First athlete to successfully land each of the six types of quadruple jumps in one program
  • His signature move "Raspberry Twist" is a nod to his last name. The origin of his last name 'malina' in Russian translates to raspberry.
  • Before each competition, he eats a chocolate bar
  • Enjoys skateboarding, parkour and tricking, painting and drawing, and video games
  • Speaks fluent Russian
Biographical Information

Ilia Malinin began skating at age six, inspired by his parents, Tatyana Malinina and Roman Skornyakov, both former Olympic figure skaters for Uzbekistan. His early exposure to the sport included his grandfather, a former figure skater for the USSR and youth figure skating coach in Siberia, training him while babysitting him at the ice rink and laid a foundation for his meteoric rise in the figure skating world.

After a second place at U.S. nationals and being named an alternate for the Beijing Winter Olympics, Malininwon gold at the 2022 World Junior Championships and previewing his soon-to-be senior-level brilliance. In September 2022, Malinin became the first skater to successfully land the quadruple Axel in an international competition. Later that season, Malinin earned a bronze medal at the 2023 ISU Figure Skating World Championships, and followed by placing first at the 2024 event, highlighting his technical mastery and marking the fastest turnaround from junior to senior world champion in 26 years. He then defended his gold medal in 2025, becoming the first American skater to win back-to-back titles since Nathan Chen's three-peat from 2018-21.

Malinin's signature quad jumps have earned him the nickname "QuadGod" on the ice but his skating is also known for a contemporary feel using music selections from rapper NF, rock band Falling in Reverse, and the soundtrack from the HBO show Succession as part of his previous routines. He also utilizes backflips and his signature move "Raspberry Twist" as fun elements to captivate the audience and judges.

World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – gold (Singles - Men)
  • Years of participation: Singles - Men 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025
  • Medals: 3 (2 gold, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 2025 (Singles - Men); 2024 (Singles - Men)
    • Bronze – 2023 (Singles - Men)
Career Highlights
  • World Junior Championships, Tallinn, 2022 (Singles - Men)
  • World Junior Championships, Tallinn, 2020 (Singles - Men)
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 gold)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Mixed), 8th (Singles - Men)

content_tags.title: Olympic Winter Games Milano Cortina 2026, Qualified

bio.biography:
About:

Jordan currently lives in Seattle with his wife Shannon and two boys, Kai & Laith. He still gets out on the water from time to time. He manages the flagship office of the top real estate brokerage in the Northwest.

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Athens 2004, 14th (C2 1000m - Men, C2 500m - Men)
    • Olympic Games Sydney 2000, 16th (C1 1000m - Men), 17th (C1 500m - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2007 – 10th (C1 1000m - Men), 18th (C1 500m - Men)
  • Years of participation: C1 1000m - Men 2002, 2003, 2006, 2007; C1 500m - Men 2003, 2006, 2007; C2 1000m - Men 2001, 2005; C2 200m - Men 2001, 2005; C2 500m - Men 2001, 2005; C1 200m - Men 2002, 2003; C4 200m - Men 2001
  • Top finish: 10th – 2007 (C1 1000m - Men)

Career Highlights
  • World Junior Championships, Chiesa in Valmalenco, 2017 (Skicross - Men)
  • World Junior Championships, Val Thorens, 2016 (Skicross - Men)
  • World Junior Championships, Chiesa in Valmalenco, 2015 (Skicross - Men)
Quick Facts
  • Son of Ron Malm and Tomi Bortolazzo
  • Has one sibling, Randy
  • Competed in alpine skiing for 14 years before transitioning to skicross in 2010
  • Hobbies include mountain biking, snowmobiling, flying and photography.

Quick Facts
  • Son of Kyle Malone and Latasha Sturdivant
  • Has a sister, Zion
  • His father, Kyle, was a former collegiate sprinter
  • Diagnosed with Leber’s hereditary optic neuropathy, a rare eye condition that leaves him with 20/400 vision in his left eye and 20/600 in his right eye
  • Started losing his vision at 2015 and went to the optometrist, who diagnosed him with the genetic disorder
  • Split his time between Hamilton Southeastern High School and the Indiana School for the Blind and Visually Impaired
  • 2019 Indiana High School Athletic Association (IHSAA) 200m state champion
  • Holds several records at Hamilton Southeastern High School, including the 100-meter, 200-meter, and 4x100 relay
  • His 100-meter time of 10.80 at the age of 15 would have won him a bronze medal at the 2016 Paralympic Games
  • Has been a keynote speaker at TEDx Indianapolis
  • Became one of the only legally blind Division I sprinters in the nation at Indiana State University
  • Enjoys running, painting, taking pictures.
Paralympic Experience
  • 2-time Paralympian; 6-time Paralympic medalist (2 gold, 3 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, gold (100m T12 - Men), silver (400m T12 - Men), bronze (Universal Relay - Open)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (4 x 100m Medley - Mixed), silver (100m T12 - Men, 400m T12 - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2024 – (Universal Relay - Mixed), silver (100m T12 - Men)
  • Years of participation: 100m T12 - Men 2019, 2023, 2024; Universal Relay - Mixed 2019, 2023, 2024
  • Medals: 3 (2 gold, 1 silver)
    • Gold – 2023 (100m T12 - Men); 2019 (Universal Relay - Mixed)
    • Silver – 2024 (100m T12 - Men)
